"anything else?"

Yep, slashing conditions are programmable.

Every AVS can define its own rules for what counts as a doing things wrong. It could be anything, depending on what they’re building.

"tldr"

Slashing is the penalty given to people who help secure EigenLayer’s AVSs if they don’t do their job properly.

In practice, they lose part of their staked ETH when they misbehave.

"bro, wat is wayfinder"

An explanation of 
@AIWayfinder
, in (very) simple terms.

"ok"

Every time the agent uses a route successfully, validators are rewarded.

The more paths that are added and validated, the better the agent becomes.

In short, the community ensures the agent keeps suggesting safe routes and optimizations.

"tldr"

Think of Wayfinder as a hub for AI agents that take care of all your DeFi and blockchain needs, from yield optimization to smart contract coding.

The agents suggest optimizations through wayfinding paths, which are secured by validators.

"cool and?"

Anyone can then earn a fixed yield on their assets, but also buy yield, just like your friends did with your apples.

The coolest part is that Pendle makes those principal and yield tokens liquid, meaning you can sell them anytime.

"tldr"

Pendle splits yield-bearing tokens into two: principal and yield tokens. Users can earn a fixed and guaranteed yield on their assets, while traders can bet on yield fluctuations.

Also, these tokens are liquid, so you can sell them whenever you want.

"yes?"

DoubleZero creates another water pipe. Only high-quality data (like your transaction) can flow through this pipe, avoiding congestion in other pipes.

Cool thing? People with unused dark fiber can add it to the pipe (unused fiber cables), increasing decentralization.

"tldr"

DoubleZero creates a fast, priority lane for data transmission. Instead of competing with the broader internet, blockchains send and receive data directly through DoubleZero.

It’s like a special internet made for filtered blockchain data.

"bro, wat is expressivity"

An explanation of expressivity, in (very) simple terms.

"who dis?"

All chains are expressive to a certain degree. Bitcoin not much, Ethereum a bit more and some even more.

A good example is 
@fluentxyz
 with blended execution. Instead of having to choose between Solana, Ethereum or web2 tools, they blend all of them in the same place

"tldr"

Expressivity is about the freedom developers have to build applications the way they want, using tools they're already familiar with and optimized for their use cases.

Tools that increase expressivity include programming languages, libraries, compilers, etc.

"examples?"
@celestia
 uses Succinct to secure its data attestation bridge, aka Blobstream. (You can read more about it below.)

Some OP Stack rollups, like 
@Mantle_Official
, use Succinct to generate ZK proofs.

But in theory, any app could use Succinct to generate any proof.

"tldr?"

Succinct allows applications to request proofs from provers, who will generate and verify them on their behalf for a small fee.

Cool part? No need to use a complex programming language for proving, Rust works perfectly fine.

"but succinct?"

Remember, you want to prove "something". That’s vague on purpose.

Every app needs proofs: ZK rollups, bridges, games, oracles, AI agents. Whether it’s proving that assets were bridged correctly or that an AI agent followed the right logic, they need proofs.

An explanation of zkTLS, in (very) simple terms.

"then?"

Another server or user can verify the proof and use it to confirm that you have a good reputation without needing to share your eBay account.

The best part is that they don’t see the original data, just a cryptographic guarantee that it’s true.

"tldr?"

This answer will also be encrypted and sent back to you, but this time in a readable format.
